title: Mac Running Slowly
drDigitalIntro: A slow Mac often has a simple cause. Too many apps open at once = less memory, slower performance. A full hard drive (less than 10% free) also slows things down. Check storage in Apple menu > About This Mac > Storage. Close apps you aren't using. Delete large files you don't need. Restart your Mac — this clears the memory. Old Macs also run newer software harder; check Activity Monitor to see what's using CPU.

playgroundTask.question: Your Mac has been on for two weeks without a restart and is running slowly. What is the most likely fix?

options.text: Buy more RAM — the Mac is permanently broken
Restart the Mac — it clears memory and fixes many slowdowns

drDigitalSuccess: Restarting clears temporary files and frees memory — it's the single best fix for a gradual slowdown.
drDigitalHint: What happens to memory and temporary files when you restart a computer that's been running for weeks?
